title: Ambiguous error if an external virtual class function is used instead of a local function in a `let` statement

features.name: let statement with virtual class static function call via scope resolution
features.description: Code should include a let statement that calls a virtual class static function using scope resolution operator (ClassName#(...)::function_name)
features.snippet:
virtual class C#(int N = 1); static function byte o_t mm(input byte i[N][N]); return o; endfunction endclass
let arr = C#(1)::mm('{'{1}});

features.name: function returning typedef 2D unpacked array
features.description: Code should include a function that returns a typedef'd 2D unpacked array type (e.g., byte array[N][N])
features.snippet: typedef byte o_t[N][N]; function o_t mm(input byte i[N][N]); o_t o; return o; endfunction

features.name: nested array literal in let statement
features.description: Code should include a nested array literal with apostrophe prefix syntax ('{'{'{'...'}}}') used in a let statement

features.name: virtual class with static function
features.description: Code should include a virtual class containing a static function with typedef parameter and return types
features.snippet: virtual class C#(int N = 1); typedef byte o_t[N][N]; static function o_t mm(input byte i[N][N]); endfunction endclass
